channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: The Iranian Striker: The journey of Mehdi Taremi | Al Jazeera Originals
item.description: From Bushehr to the world stage, Mehdi Taremi reflects on football, sacrifice and representing Iran.

item.title: World Cup: Which teams made the quarterfinals, and what’s the schedule?
item.description: Complete FIFA World Cup 2026 quarterfinals lineup, with match start times and venue locations inside the United States.

item.title: Why have half a million Russians gone bankrupt amid Ukraine war?
item.description: European intelligence report warns of strain on Russia's banks, but experts say a full-blown crisis is unlikely

item.title: World Cup 2026 quarterfinals: Full schedule and Egypt FIFA dispute
item.description: World Cup quarterfinals are set, with eight teams advancing as Egypt’s FIFA controversy and VAR decisions prompt debate.

item.title: Funeral events for Iran’s slain Supreme Leader Khamenei under way in Iraq
item.description: Khamenei's coffin made its way through the streets of Najaf to the shrine of Imam Ali, Prophet Muhammad's son-in-law.

item.title: Egypt coach condemns silence on killing of Palestinian children
item.description: Egypt coach condemns silence on killing of Palestinian children

item.title: ‘Where do I get that from?’: UK asylum seekers face hefty repayment bill
item.description: Refugees and NGOs decry the government's plans to make people pay 10,000 pounds ($13,360) towards subsistence fees.

item.title: Australian World Cup star Mabil fires back at far-right politician
item.description: Australian World Cup star Mabil fires back at far-right politician

item.title: NATO summit live: Trump, world leaders meet in Turkiye’s Ankara
item.description: The second and final day of the key gathering is under way in Turkiye's capital.

item.title: Iran’s economy faces long road to recovery as fragile truce tested
item.description: The devastation that industrial facilities have taken from two wars in a year might take years to reverse, say analysts.

item.title: Egypt fans react to emotional World Cup exit after unforgettable campaign
item.description: Al Jazeera spoke with Egypt fans gathered at Obour City to watch the FIFA World Cup Round of 16 match against Argentina.

item.title: France, Mbappe play Morocco in FIFA World Cup quarterfinal: What we know
item.description: Key talking points as Kylian Mbappe leads France into a rematch after Les Bleus beat Morocco in the 2022 World Cup.

item.title: At UN, Cuba rallies support against ‘ruthless’ US blockade
item.description: Diplomatic tensions between Washington and Havana have widened in recent months.

item.title: Childhood on hold: How Gaza’s children spend a summer without play
item.description: Gaza mothers describe a third wartime summer where children carry adult burdens, deprived of play and art.

item.title: Syria grapples with unrest as government faces old and new threats
item.description: Damascus shaken by twin blasts during Macron's visit, marking heightened threats to Syria's fragile stability

item.title: Argentina fan waves Israeli flag at World Cup match with Egypt
item.description: An Argentina fan waves the Israeli flag during his team’s Round of 16 match against Egypt.

item.title: Khamenei’s coffin arrives in Iraq as US strikes hit Iran
item.description: The coffin carrying Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei has arrived in one of Shia Islam’s holiest cities, Najaf.

item.title: 99% of people on Earth will get sunlight at the same time this Wednesday
item.description: On July 8, at about 11:10 GMT, about 99 percent of the world experiences sunlight at the same time.

item.title: Triumph to disaster: Egypt’s pride remains as they bow out of World Cup
item.description: Proud Egyptians celebrate their team's historic run at the World Cup but blame bias for a heartbreaking exit.

item.title: Oil surges as US strikes Iran, reversing return to pre-war prices
item.description: Brent crude rises above $76 a barrel for the first time in two weeks amid renewed violence in Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: IOC eases restrictions on Russian athletes ahead of 2028 games
item.description: The International Olympic Committee has lifted some of the restrictions on Russian athletes.

item.title: FIFA condemns fan’s racist attack on IShowSpeed at Argentina World Cup match
item.description: An Argentina supporter told Speed to 'go cry at the zoo' on his livestream at the match against Cape Verde in Miami.

item.title: Experts warn Trump is playing politics with US intelligence
item.description: Former officials warn that Trump's intelligence overhaul risks politicising national security.

item.title: VAR call, Argentina win over Egypt raise questions of legitimacy at World Cup
item.description: Experts question political influence and the need to keep Lionel Messi's side in the World Cup amid Egypt's heartbreak.

item.title: US immigration officer shoots and kills man in Texas
item.description: Man, identified as Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, is latest to be killed by ICE officers since President Trump took power.
